But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Tsukinooka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] |image_caption = Mound (right: front part, left: rear circular part) |alternate_name = Tsukinooka Kofun |affiliation = Wakamiya Kofun Group |location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ukiha|ja|うきは市}}, Yoshii Town, Wakamiya 358-1 (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine grounds) |coordinates = 33.34501 |coordinates = 130.76544 |omitted = JP-40 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |length = 80m (95m including base terrace)
Height: 7m (rear circular part) |burial = Rear circular part: {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}
(containing {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}})
Constriction area: Unknown 1
Front part: Unknown 1 |burial goods = 8 sets of armor, bronze mirrors, ornaments, horse fittings, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = (Hypothesized) {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣}} clan |imperial_tomb = |historic_sites_status = None |tangible_cultural_properties = burial goods (National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}) |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka |type = Zenpokoenfun-W |map_dot_label = Tsukinooka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukinooka Kofun''' (月岡古墳, also Tsukinooka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Yoshii Town, {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|ja|うきは市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mound). While not Historic Site1 as a historic site, its burial goods are recognized as a National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Wakamiya Kofun Group !Kofun name !colspan=2|Mound type !burial !built !Historic Status !notes |- |'''Tsukinooka Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||95m||{{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}
({{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}})||Mid-5th century||None||burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 |- |{{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||91m||Two horizontal stone chambers||Late 5th century||None|| |- |{{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||74m||One horizontal stone chamber||Early 6th century||National Historic Site||Decorated kofun |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳}}. Currently, the mound lies within the grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, with a shrine building (covering the stone sarcophagus) at its peak. The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} Construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|Helmet fragment
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鉄鏃 (橿考研保管).JPG|Iron arrowheads
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} == Related Facilities == * Yoshii History and Folklore Museum (Ukiha, Fukuoka) – Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukinooka Kofun. == See Also == * Wakamiya Kofun Group ''' [[Tsukadō Kofun]] ''' [[Hioka Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{National Important designation1 Database|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳出土品}} * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Wakamiya Kofun Group (Hioka, Tsukinooka, Tsukadō)] – Ukiha City Website revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Tsukinooka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] |image_caption = Mound (right: front part, left: rear circular part) |alternate_name = Tsukinooka Kofun |affiliation = Wakamiya Kofun Group |location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Ukiha|ja|うきは市}}, Yoshii Town, Wakamiya 358-1 (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine grounds) |coordinates = 33.34501 |coordinates = 130.76544 |omitted = JP-40 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |length = 80m (95m including base terrace)
Height: 7m (rear circular part) |burial = Rear circular part: {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}
(containing {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}})
Constriction area: Unknown 1
Front part: Unknown 1 |burial goods = 8 sets of armor, bronze mirrors, ornaments, horse fittings, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = (Hypothesized) {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣}} clan |imperial_tomb = |historic_sites_status = None |tangible_cultural_properties = burial goods (National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}) |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka |type = Zenpokoenfun-W |map_dot_label = Tsukinooka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukinooka Kofun''' (月岡古墳, also Tsukinooka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Yoshii Town, {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|ja|うきは市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mound). While not Historic Site1 as a historic site, its burial goods are recognized as a National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Wakamiya Kofun Group !Kofun name !colspan=2|Mound type !burial !built !Historic Status !notes |- |'''Tsukinooka Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||95m||{{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}
({{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}})||Mid-5th century||None||burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 |- |{{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||91m||Two horizontal stone chambers||Late 5th century||None|| |- |{{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||74m||One horizontal stone chamber||Early 6th century||National Historic Site||Decorated kofun |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳}}. Currently, the mound lies within the grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, with a shrine building (covering the stone sarcophagus) at its peak. The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} Construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|Helmet fragment
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鉄鏃 (橿考研保管).JPG|Iron arrowheads
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} == Related Facilities == * Yoshii History and Folklore Museum (Ukiha, Fukuoka) – Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukinooka Kofun. == See Also == * Wakamiya Kofun Group ''' [[Tsukadō Kofun]] ''' [[Hioka Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{National Important designation1 Database|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳出土品}} * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Wakamiya Kofun Group (Hioka, Tsukinooka, Tsukadō)] – Ukiha City Website of Japan]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[ja:月岡古墳]] [[en:Tsukioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukioka Kofun}}

Front part: Unknown 1 |burial goods = 8 sets of armor, bronze mirrors, ornaments, horse fittings, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |satellite_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |buried_person = (Hypothesized) {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft}} clan |imperial_tomb = |historic_sites_status = None |tangible_cultural_properties = burial goods (National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}) |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka |type = Zenpokoenfun-W |map_dot_label = Tsukinooka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukinooka Kofun''' (月岡古墳, also Tsukinooka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Yoshii Town, {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|ja|うきは市|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mound). While not Historic Site1 as a historic site, its burial goods are recognized as a National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Wakamiya Kofun Group !Kofun name !colspan=2|Mound type !burial !built !Historic Status !notes |- |'''Tsukinooka Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||95m||{{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
({{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}})||Mid-5th century||None||burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 |- |{{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||91m||Two horizontal stone chambers||Late 5th century||None|| |- |{{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||74m||One horizontal stone chamber||Early 6th century||National Historic Site||Decorated kofun |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川|12=draft}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}}. Currently, the mound lies within the grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, with a shrine building (covering the stone sarcophagus) at its peak. The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} Construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|12=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|Helmet fragment
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鉄鏃 (橿考研保管).JPG|Iron arrowheads
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} == Related Facilities == * Yoshii History and Folklore Museum (Ukiha, Fukuoka) – Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukinooka Kofun. == See Also == * Wakamiya Kofun Group ''' [[Tsukadō Kofun]] ''' [[Hioka Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{National Important designation1 Database|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳出土品}} * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Wakamiya Kofun Group (Hioka, Tsukinooka, Tsukadō)] – Ukiha City Website of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[ja:月岡古墳]] [[en:Tsukioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukioka Kofun}}

The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|12=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Constriction area: Unknown 1
Front part: Unknown 1 |burial goods = 8 sets of armor, bronze mirrors, ornaments, horse fittings, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |satellite_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |buried_person = (Hypothesized) {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft}} clan |imperial_tomb = |historic_sites_status = None |tangible_cultural_properties = burial goods (National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}) |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka |type = Zenpokoenfun-W |map_dot_label = Tsukinooka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukinooka Kofun''' (月岡古墳, also Tsukinooka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Yoshii Town, {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|ja|うきは市|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mound). While not Historic Site1 as a historic site, its burial goods are recognized as a National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Wakamiya Kofun Group !Kofun name !colspan=2|Mound type !burial !built !Historic Status !notes |- |'''Tsukinooka Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||95m||{{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
({{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}})||Mid-5th century||None||burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 |- |{{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||91m||Two horizontal stone chambers||Late 5th century||None|| |- |{{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||74m||One horizontal stone chamber||Early 6th century||National Historic Site||Decorated kofun |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川|12=draft}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}}. Currently, the mound lies within the grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, with a shrine building (covering the stone sarcophagus) at its peak. The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} Construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|12=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Tsukinooka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] |image_caption = Mound (right: front part, left: rear circular part) |alternate_name = Tsukinooka Kofun |affiliation = Wakamiya Kofun Group |location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukiha|ja|うきは市|12=draft}}, Yoshii Town, Wakamiya 358-1 (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine grounds) |coordinates = 33.34501 |coordinates = 130.76544 |omitted = JP-40 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |length = 80m (95m including base terrace)
Height: 7m (rear circular part) |burial = Rear circular part: {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(containing {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}})
Constriction area: Unknown 1
Front part: Unknown 1 |burial goods = 8 sets of armor, bronze mirrors, ornaments, horse fittings, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |satellite_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |buried_person = (Hypothesized) {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft}} clan |imperial_tomb = |historic_sites_status = None |tangible_cultural_properties = burial goods (National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}) |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka |type = Zenpokoenfun-W |map_dot_label = Tsukinooka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukinooka Kofun''' (月岡古墳, also Tsukinooka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Yoshii Town, {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|ja|うきは市|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mound). While not Historic Site1 as a historic site, its burial goods are recognized as a National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Wakamiya Kofun Group !Kofun name !colspan=2|Mound type !burial !built !Historic Status !notes |- |'''Tsukinooka Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||95m||{{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
({{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}})||Mid-5th century||None||burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 |- |{{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||91m||Two horizontal stone chambers||Late 5th century||None|| |- |{{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||74m||One horizontal stone chamber||Early 6th century||National Historic Site||Decorated kofun |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川|12=draft}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}}. Currently, the mound lies within the grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, with a shrine building (covering the stone sarcophagus) at its peak. The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} Construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|12=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|Helmet fragment
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鉄鏃 (橿考研保管).JPG|Iron arrowheads
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} == Related Facilities == * Yoshii History and Folklore Museum (Ukiha, Fukuoka) – Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukinooka Kofun. == See Also == * Wakamiya Kofun Group ''' [[Tsukadō Kofun]] ''' [[Hioka Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{National Important designation1 Database|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳出土品}} * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Wakamiya Kofun Group (Hioka, Tsukinooka, Tsukadō)] – Ukiha City Website of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[ja:月岡古墳]] [[en:Tsukioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukioka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川|12=draft}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}}. Currently, the mound lies within the grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, with a shrine building (covering the stone sarcophagus) at its peak. The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} Construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|12=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|Helmet fragment
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鉄鏃 (橿考研保管).JPG|Iron arrowheads
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} == Related Facilities == * Yoshii History and Folklore Museum (Ukiha, Fukuoka) – Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukinooka Kofun. == See Also == * Wakamiya Kofun Group ''' [[Tsukadō Kofun]] ''' [[Hioka Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{National Important designation1 Database|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳出土品}} * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Wakamiya Kofun Group (Hioka, Tsukinooka, Tsukadō)] – Ukiha City Website of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[ja:月岡古墳]] [[en:Tsukioka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukioka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Tsukinooka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] |image_caption = Mound (right: front part, left: rear circular part) |alternate_name = Tsukinooka Kofun |affiliation = Wakamiya Kofun Group |location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukiha|ja|うきは市|12=draft}}, Yoshii Town, Wakamiya 358-1 (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine grounds) |coordinates = 33.34501 |coordinates = 130.76544 |omitted = JP-40 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |length = 80m (95m including base terrace)
Height: 7m (rear circular part) |burial = Rear circular part: {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(containing {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}})
Constriction area: Unknown 1
Front part: Unknown 1 |burial goods = 8 sets of armor, bronze mirrors, ornaments, horse fittings, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |satellite_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |buried_person = (Hypothesized) {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft}} clan |imperial_tomb = |historic_sites_status = None |tangible_cultural_properties = burial goods (National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}) |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka |type = Zenpokoenfun-W |map_dot_label = Tsukinooka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukinooka Kofun''' (月岡古墳, also Tsukinooka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Yoshii Town, {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|ja|うきは市|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mound). While not Historic Site1 as a historic site, its burial goods are recognized as a National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Wakamiya Kofun Group !Kofun name !colspan=2|Mound type !burial !built !Historic Status !notes |- |'''Tsukinooka Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||95m||{{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
({{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}})||Mid-5th century||None||burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 |- |{{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||91m||Two horizontal stone chambers||Late 5th century||None|| |- |{{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||74m||One horizontal stone chamber||Early 6th century||National Historic Site||Decorated kofun |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川|12=draft}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}}. Currently, the mound lies within the grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, with a shrine building (covering the stone sarcophagus) at its peak. The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} Construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|12=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|Helmet fragment
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鉄鏃 (橿考研保管).JPG|Iron arrowheads
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} == Related Facilities == * Yoshii History and Folklore Museum (Ukiha, Fukuoka) – Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukinooka Kofun. == See Also == * Wakamiya Kofun Group ''' [[Tsukadō Kofun]] ''' [[Hioka Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{National Important designation1 Database|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳出土品}} * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Wakamiya Kofun Group (Hioka, Tsukinooka, Tsukadō)] – Ukiha City Website of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[ja:月岡古墳]] [[en:Tsukioka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Tsukioka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Tsukinooka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] |image_caption = Mound (right: front part, left: rear circular part) |alternate_name = Tsukinooka Kofun |affiliation = Wakamiya Kofun Group |location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukiha|ja|うきは市|12=draft}}, Yoshii Town, Wakamiya 358-1 (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine grounds) |coordinates = 33.34501 |coordinates = 130.76544 |omitted = JP-40 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |length = 80m (95m including base terrace)
Height: 7m (rear circular part) |burial = Rear circular part: {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(containing {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}})
Constriction area: Unknown 1
Front part: Unknown 1 |burial goods = 8 sets of armor, bronze mirrors, ornaments, horse fittings, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |satellite_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |buried_person = (Hypothesized) {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft}} clan |imperial_tomb = |historic_sites_status = None |tangible_cultural_properties = burial goods (National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}) |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka |type = Zenpokoenfun-W |map_dot_label = Tsukinooka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukinooka Kofun''' (月岡古墳, also Tsukinooka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Yoshii Town, {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|ja|うきは市|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mound). While not Historic Site1 as a historic site, its burial goods are recognized as a National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Wakamiya Kofun Group !Kofun name !colspan=2|Mound type !burial !built !Historic Status !notes |- |'''Tsukinooka Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||95m||{{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
({{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}})||Mid-5th century||None||burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 |- |{{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||91m||Two horizontal stone chambers||Late 5th century||None|| |- |{{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||74m||One horizontal stone chamber||Early 6th century||National Historic Site||Decorated kofun |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川|12=draft}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}}. Currently, the mound lies within the grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, with a shrine building (covering the stone sarcophagus) at its peak. The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} Construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|12=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|Helmet fragment
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鉄鏃 (橿考研保管).JPG|Iron arrowheads
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} == Related Facilities == * Yoshii History and Folklore Museum (Ukiha, Fukuoka) – Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukinooka Kofun. == See Also == * Wakamiya Kofun Group ''' [[Tsukadō Kofun]] ''' [[Hioka Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{National Important designation1 Database|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳出土品}} * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Wakamiya Kofun Group (Hioka, Tsukinooka, Tsukadō)] – Ukiha City Website of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[ja:月岡古墳]] [[en:Tsukioka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Tsukioka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} Construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|12=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|Helmet fragment
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鉄鏃 (橿考研保管).JPG|Iron arrowheads
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} == Related Facilities == * Yoshii History and Folklore Museum (Ukiha, Fukuoka) – Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukinooka Kofun. == See Also == * Wakamiya Kofun Group ''' [[Tsukadō Kofun]] ''' [[Hioka Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{National Important designation1 Database|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳出土品}} * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Wakamiya Kofun Group (Hioka, Tsukinooka, Tsukadō)] – Ukiha City Website of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[ja:月岡古墳]] [[en:Tsukioka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Tsukioka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Tsukinooka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] |image_caption = Mound (right: front part, left: rear circular part) |alternate_name = Tsukinooka Kofun |affiliation = Wakamiya Kofun Group |location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukiha|ja|うきは市|12=draft}}, Yoshii Town, Wakamiya 358-1 (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine grounds) |coordinates = 33.34501 |coordinates = 130.76544 |omitted = JP-40 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |length = 80m (95m including base terrace)
Height: 7m (rear circular part) |burial = Rear circular part: {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(containing {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}})
Constriction area: Unknown 1
Front part: Unknown 1 |burial goods = 8 sets of armor, bronze mirrors, ornaments, horse fittings, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |satellite_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |buried_person = (Hypothesized) {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft}} clan |imperial_tomb = |historic_sites_status = None |tangible_cultural_properties = burial goods (National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}) |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka |type = Zenpokoenfun-W |map_dot_label = Tsukinooka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukinooka Kofun''' (月岡古墳, also Tsukinooka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Yoshii Town, {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|ja|うきは市|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mound). While not Historic Site1 as a historic site, its burial goods are recognized as a National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Wakamiya Kofun Group !Kofun name !colspan=2|Mound type !burial !built !Historic Status !notes |- |'''Tsukinooka Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||95m||{{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
({{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}})||Mid-5th century||None||burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 |- |{{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||91m||Two horizontal stone chambers||Late 5th century||None|| |- |{{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||74m||One horizontal stone chamber||Early 6th century||National Historic Site||Decorated kofun |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川|12=draft}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft}}. Currently, the mound lies within the grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, with a shrine building (covering the stone sarcophagus) at its peak. The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} Construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|12=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Tsukinooka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] |image_caption = Mound (right: front part, left: rear circular part) |alternate_name = Tsukinooka Kofun |affiliation = Wakamiya Kofun Group |location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukiha|ja|うきは市|12=draft}}, Yoshii Town, Wakamiya 358-1 (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine grounds) |coordinates = 33.34501 |coordinates = 130.76544 |omitted = JP-40 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |length = 80m (95m including base terrace)
