({{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}})||Mid-5th century||None||burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 |- |{{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||91m||Two horizontal stone chambers||Late 5th century||None|| |- |{{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||74m||One horizontal stone chamber||Early 6th century||National Historic Site||Decorated kofun |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川|12=draft|12=draft}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. Currently, the mound lies within the grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, with a shrine building (covering the stone sarcophagus) at its peak. The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} built is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft|12=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft|12=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|Helmet fragment
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鉄鏃 (橿考研保管).JPG|Iron arrowheads
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} == Related Facilities == * Yoshii History and Folklore Museum (Ukiha, Fukuoka) – Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukinooka Kofun. == See Also == * Wakamiya Kofun Group ''' [[Tsukadō Kofun]] ''' [[Hioka Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{National Important designation1 Database|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳Excavated Artifacts}} * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Wakamiya Kofun Group (Hioka, Tsukinooka, Tsukadō)] – Ukiha City Website of Japan]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukioka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[ja:月岡古墳]] [[en:Tsukioka Kofun]]

The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft|12=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft|12=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|Helmet fragment
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鉄鏃 (橿考研保管).JPG|Iron arrowheads
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} == Related Facilities == * Yoshii History and Folklore Museum (Ukiha, Fukuoka) – Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukinooka Kofun. == See Also == * Wakamiya Kofun Group ''' [[Tsukadō Kofun]] ''' [[Hioka Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{National Important designation1 Database|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳Excavated Artifacts}} * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Wakamiya Kofun Group (Hioka, Tsukinooka, Tsukadō)] – Ukiha City Website of Japan]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukioka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[ja:月岡古墳]] [[en:Tsukioka Kofun]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Tsukinooka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] |image_caption = Mound (right: front part, left: rear circular part) |alternate_name = Tsukinooka Kofun |affiliation = Wakamiya Kofun Group |location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukiha|ja|うきは市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yoshii Town, Wakamiya 358-1 (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine grounds) |coordinates = 33.34501 |coordinates = 130.76544 |omitted = JP-40 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |length = 80m (95m including base terrace)
Height: 7m (rear circular part) |burial = Rear circular part: {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(containing {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}})
Constriction area: Unknown 1
Front part: Unknown 1 |burial goods = 8 sets of armor, bronze mirrors, ornaments, horse fittings, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |satellite_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |buried_person = (Hypothesized) {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft|12=draft}} clan |imperial_tomb = |historic_sites_status = None |tangible_cultural_properties = burial goods (National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}) |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka |type = Zenpokoenfun-W |map_dot_label = Tsukinooka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukinooka Kofun''' (月岡古墳, also Tsukinooka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Yoshii Town, {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|ja|うきは市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mound). While not Historic Site1 as a historic site, its burial goods are recognized as a National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Wakamiya Kofun Group !Kofun name !colspan=2|Mound type !burial !built !Historic Status !notes |- |'''Tsukinooka Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||95m||{{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
({{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}})||Mid-5th century||None||burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 |- |{{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||91m||Two horizontal stone chambers||Late 5th century||None|| |- |{{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||74m||One horizontal stone chamber||Early 6th century||National Historic Site||Decorated kofun |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川|12=draft|12=draft}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. Currently, the mound lies within the grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, with a shrine building (covering the stone sarcophagus) at its peak. The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} built is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft|12=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft|12=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|Helmet fragment
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鉄鏃 (橿考研保管).JPG|Iron arrowheads
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} == Related Facilities == * Yoshii History and Folklore Museum (Ukiha, Fukuoka) – Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukinooka Kofun. == See Also == * Wakamiya Kofun Group ''' [[Tsukadō Kofun]] ''' [[Hioka Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{National Important designation1 Database|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳Excavated Artifacts}} * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Wakamiya Kofun Group (Hioka, Tsukinooka, Tsukadō)] – Ukiha City Website [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukioka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[ja:月岡古墳]] [[en:Tsukioka Kofun]]

Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|Helmet fragment
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鉄鏃 (橿考研保管).JPG|Iron arrowheads
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} == Related Facilities == * Yoshii History and Folklore Museum (Ukiha, Fukuoka) – Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukinooka Kofun. == See Also == * Wakamiya Kofun Group ''' [[Tsukadō Kofun]] ''' [[Hioka Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{National Important designation1 Database|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳Excavated Artifacts}} * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Wakamiya Kofun Group (Hioka, Tsukinooka, Tsukadō)] – Ukiha City Website [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukioka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[ja:月岡古墳]] [[en:Tsukioka Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Tsukinooka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] |image_caption = Mound (right: front part, left: rear circular part) |alternate_name = Tsukinooka Kofun |affiliation = Wakamiya Kofun Group |location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukiha|ja|うきは市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yoshii Town, Wakamiya 358-1 (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine grounds) |coordinates = 33.34501 |coordinates = 130.76544 |omitted = JP-40 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |length = 80m (95m including base terrace)
Height: 7m (rear circular part) |burial = Rear circular part: {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(containing {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}})
Constriction area: Unknown 1
Front part: Unknown 1 |burial goods = 8 sets of armor, bronze mirrors, ornaments, horse fittings, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |satellite_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |buried_person = (Hypothesized) {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft|12=draft}} clan |imperial_tomb = |historic_sites_status = None |tangible_cultural_properties = burial goods (National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}) |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka |type = Zenpokoenfun-W |map_dot_label = Tsukinooka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukinooka Kofun''' (月岡古墳, also Tsukinooka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Yoshii Town, {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|ja|うきは市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mound). While not Historic Site1 as a historic site, its burial goods are recognized as a National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Wakamiya Kofun Group !Kofun name !colspan=2|Mound type !burial !built !Historic Status !notes |- |'''Tsukinooka Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||95m||{{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
({{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}})||Mid-5th century||None||burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 |- |{{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||91m||Two horizontal stone chambers||Late 5th century||None|| |- |{{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||74m||One horizontal stone chamber||Early 6th century||National Historic Site||Decorated kofun |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川|12=draft|12=draft}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. Currently, the mound lies within the grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, with a shrine building (covering the stone sarcophagus) at its peak. The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} built is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft|12=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|12=draft|12=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|Helmet fragment
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鉄鏃 (橿考研保管).JPG|Iron arrowheads
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} == Related Facilities == * Yoshii History and Folklore Museum (Ukiha, Fukuoka) – Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukinooka Kofun. == See Also == * Wakamiya Kofun Group ''' [[Tsukadō Kofun]] ''' [[Hioka Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{National Important designation1 Database|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳Excavated Artifacts}} * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Wakamiya Kofun Group (Hioka, Tsukinooka, Tsukadō)] – Ukiha City Website [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukioka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[ja:月岡古墳]] [[en:Tsukioka Kofun]]

[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} built is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically the mid-5th century. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|Helmet fragment
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鉄鏃 (橿考研保管).JPG|Iron arrowheads
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} == Related Facilities == * Yoshii History and Folklore Museum (Ukiha, Fukuoka) – Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukinooka Kofun. == See Also == * Wakamiya Kofun Group ''' [[Tsukadō Kofun]] ''' [[Hioka Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{National Important designation1 Database|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳Excavated Artifacts}} * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Wakamiya Kofun Group (Hioka, Tsukinooka, Tsukadō)] – Ukiha City Website [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukioka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[ja:月岡古墳]] [[en:Tsukioka Kofun]]

revision.comment: 22 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:月岡古墳 up to rev 102672266
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 月岡古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 月ノ岡古墳 |所属 = 若宮古墳群 |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[うきは市]]吉井町若宮358-1ほか（若宮八幡宮境内） |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 20|緯度秒 = 42.03 |経度度 = 130|経度分 = 45|経度秒 = 55.60 |ISO = JP-40 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長80m（または95m）
高さ7m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 後円部：[[竪穴式石室]]
　　　（内部に[[長持形石棺]]）
くびれ部：不明1基
前方部：不明1基 |出土品 = 甲冑8組ほか銅鏡・装身具・馬具・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = （一説）[[的氏|的臣]]一族 |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Fukuoka |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル = 月岡古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|33|20|42|N|130|46|10|E}} | zoom = 15 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|若宮古墳群分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|33|20|42.03|N|130|45|55.60|E}} | label1='''月岡古墳''' | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|33|20|40.40|N|130|46|1.92|E}} | label2=[[日岡古墳]] | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|33|20|44.65|N|130|46|24.90|E}} | label3=[[塚堂古墳]] | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size3=30 }} '''月岡古墳'''（つきのおかこふん、月ノ岡古墳）は、[[福岡県]][[うきは市]]吉井町若宮にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。若宮古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+若宮古墳群一覧 !古墳名 !colspan=2|墳丘 !埋葬施設 !築造時期 !史跡 !備考 |- |'''月岡古墳'''||前方後円墳||95m||竪穴式石室ほか
（長持形石棺）||5c中||なし||出土品は重要文化財 |- |[[塚堂古墳]]||前方後円墳||91m||横穴式石室2基||5c後半||なし|| |- |[[日岡古墳]]||前方後円墳||74m||横穴式石室||6c初頭||国史跡||装飾古墳 |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|後円部墳丘}}]] 福岡県南部、[[筑後川]]南岸の台地上に築造された古墳である。[[塚堂古墳]]・[[日岡古墳]]とともに若宮古墳群を形成する。現在は若宮八幡宮の境内に所在し、墳頂には社殿（石棺覆屋）が建てられ、墳丘南側は宅地化で改変されている。[[江戸時代]]後期に石室が発掘されて副葬品が出土し、[[1985年]]（[[昭和]]60年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、墳丘主軸を東西方向として、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘長は80メートルを測り、墳丘最下段の基壇状テラスを含めると95メートルになる。墳丘外表では[[形象埴輪]]（人物・家形・水鳥形・蓋形・甲形埴輪など）が検出されている{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘周囲には三重の周濠が巡らされ、内側の濠は水をたたえたとみられる筑後川流域装飾古墳同時公開解説シート。。埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部に阿蘇溶結凝灰岩製の[[長持形石棺]]を据えた。石室は現在失われているため詳らかでないが、石棺の内面には朱が塗布されている。江戸時代の発掘では、金銅装眉庇付冑をはじめとして銅鏡・装身具・短甲・馬具など多数の副葬品が出土している。特に甲冑は8セットが認められ、[[黒姫山古墳]]（[[大阪府]]）の24セット、[[野中古墳]]（大阪府）の11セットに次ぐ甲冑大量副葬の事例として注目される{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0036936/3_6936_up_2ui0xie8.pdf 「月岡古墳～超重要古墳の被葬者像に迫る～（耳納風土記23）」]}} 広報うきは2023年3月1日号（第416号）、うきは市役所、2023年。。なお、昭和60年の調査で前方部・くびれ部にもそれぞれ埋葬施設が確認されているが、未調査のため詳らかでない{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]中葉頃と推定される。若宮古墳群では、塚堂古墳・日岡古墳に先行して最初に築造された首長墓である。長持形石棺の使用例は九州ではほとんどなく畿内でも有力者に限られ、甲冑の大量副葬の様相も考え合わせ、朝鮮半島との交流を見据えた要衝において畿内[[ヤマト王権]]と強い結びつきを持った武人的性格の被葬者像が指摘される。特に「うきは（生葉/浮羽）」の地名から、ヤマト王権内で軍事を司り朝鮮半島外交に従事したとされる[[的氏|的臣]]（いくはのおみ、的氏）一族を被葬者に推測する説が挙げられている。 出土品は[[1961年]]（昭和36年）に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。現在では石棺は後円部墳頂の社殿（覆屋）内で神体として祀られる形で保存され、毎月第3土曜日の古墳見学時に公開されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[文化 (元号)|文化]]2年（[[1805年]]）、若宮八幡宮宮司の安元大炊による竪穴式石室の発掘。副葬品の出土（「月岡所獲古器図」に記録、現在は吉井歴史民俗資料館保管）{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}}。 * [[嘉永]]6年（[[1853年]]）の矢野一貞『筑後将士軍談』に出土品の図示{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1925年]]（[[大正]]14年）刊行の『史蹟名勝天然紀念物調査報告書』第1輯で報告。 * [[1961年]]（[[昭和]]36年）2月17日、出土品が国の[[重要文化財]]に指定。 * [[1985年]]（昭和60年）、発掘調査。三重周濠を確認（吉井町教育委員会、[[1989年]]に報告）。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り史跡説明板。。 * 墳丘長：80メートル - 墳丘最下段の基壇状テラスを含めた場合は95メートル。 * 後円部 高さ：7メートル * 前方部 高さ：6メートル Tsukinooka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後円部墳頂の石棺覆屋 Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|後円部墳丘入り口 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === [[ファイル:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|月岡宮掘開記}}{{small|吉井歴史民俗資料館展示。}}]] * 筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。所有者は八幡神社、吉井歴史民俗資料館保管。1961年（昭和36年）2月17日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9830|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳出土品}}。 ** 金銅装眉庇付鉄胄 1頭 ** 金銅装臑当形鉄製品 一括 ** 金銅帯金具残闕 一括 ** 金銅胡簶残闕 1具分 ** 銅鏡 4面 ** 埴輪家残闕 1箇分 ** 附 発掘関係書類 1冊 === その他 === 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑
{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館保管、同企画展示時に撮影。}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|鋲留眉庇付冑片
{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館保管、同企画展示時に撮影。}} 月岡古墳 鉄鏃 (橿考研保管).JPG|鉄鏃
{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館保管、同企画展示時に撮影。}} == 関連施設 == * 吉井歴史民俗資料館（うきは市吉井町） - 月岡古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（うきは市教育委員会、2005年設置） * 筑後川流域装飾古墳同時公開解説シート * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[小林三郎 (考古学者)|小林三郎]]|year=1989|chapter=月岡古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=若宮古墳群|title=福岡県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]41|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490417|ref={{Harvid|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1925|chapter=日ノ岡、月ノ岡古墳|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/88785 史蹟名勝天然紀念物調査報告書]|volume=第1輯|publisher=福岡県|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1977|chapter=|title=吉井町誌|volume=第1巻|publisher=吉井町|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=月岡古墳 国指定重要文化財出土図録|series=|publisher=吉井町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=若宮古墳群1 -月岡古墳・塚堂古墳・日岡古墳-|series=吉井町文化財調査報告書第4集|publisher=吉井町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|title=若宮古墳群3 -月岡古墳-|series=吉井町文化財調査報告書第19集|publisher=吉井町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * 若宮古墳群 ** [[塚堂古墳]] ** [[日岡古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9830|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳出土品}} * [https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=1&id=68 筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳出土品] - 福岡県教育庁教育総務部文化財保護課「福岡県の文化財」 * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html 若宮古墳群（日岡・月岡・塚堂古墳）] - うきは市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:つきのおかこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:うきは市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:福岡県の重要文化財]]

（長持形石棺）||5c中||なし||出土品は重要文化財 |- |[[塚堂古墳]]||前方後円墳||91m||横穴式石室2基||5c後半||なし|| |- |[[日岡古墳]]||前方後円墳||74m||横穴式石室||6c初頭||国史跡||装飾古墳 |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|後円部墳丘}}]] 福岡県南部、[[筑後川]]南岸の台地上に築造された古墳である。[[塚堂古墳]]・[[日岡古墳]]とともに若宮古墳群を形成する。現在は若宮八幡宮の境内に所在し、墳頂には社殿（石棺覆屋）が建てられ、墳丘南側は宅地化で改変されている。[[江戸時代]]後期に石室が発掘されて副葬品が出土し、[[1985年]]（[[昭和]]60年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、墳丘主軸を東西方向として、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘長は80メートルを測り、墳丘最下段の基壇状テラスを含めると95メートルになる。墳丘外表では[[形象埴輪]]（人物・家形・水鳥形・蓋形・甲形埴輪など）が検出されている{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘周囲には三重の周濠が巡らされ、内側の濠は水をたたえたとみられる筑後川流域装飾古墳同時公開解説シート。。埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部に阿蘇溶結凝灰岩製の[[長持形石棺]]を据えた。石室は現在失われているため詳らかでないが、石棺の内面には朱が塗布されている。江戸時代の発掘では、金銅装眉庇付冑をはじめとして銅鏡・装身具・短甲・馬具など多数の副葬品が出土している。特に甲冑は8セットが認められ、[[黒姫山古墳]]（[[大阪府]]）の24セット、[[野中古墳]]（大阪府）の11セットに次ぐ甲冑大量副葬の事例として注目される{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0036936/3_6936_up_2ui0xie8.pdf 「月岡古墳～超重要古墳の被葬者像に迫る～（耳納風土記23）」]}} 広報うきは2023年3月1日号（第416号）、うきは市役所、2023年。。なお、昭和60年の調査で前方部・くびれ部にもそれぞれ埋葬施設が確認されているが、未調査のため詳らかでない{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]中葉頃と推定される。若宮古墳群では、塚堂古墳・日岡古墳に先行して最初に築造された首長墓である。長持形石棺の使用例は九州ではほとんどなく畿内でも有力者に限られ、甲冑の大量副葬の様相も考え合わせ、朝鮮半島との交流を見据えた要衝において畿内[[ヤマト王権]]と強い結びつきを持った武人的性格の被葬者像が指摘される。特に「うきは（生葉/浮羽）」の地名から、ヤマト王権内で軍事を司り朝鮮半島外交に従事したとされる[[的氏|的臣]]（いくはのおみ、的氏）一族を被葬者に推測する説が挙げられている。 出土品は[[1961年]]（昭和36年）に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。現在では石棺は後円部墳頂の社殿（覆屋）内で神体として祀られる形で保存され、毎月第3土曜日の古墳見学時に公開されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[文化 (元号)|文化]]2年（[[1805年]]）、若宮八幡宮宮司の安元大炊による竪穴式石室の発掘。副葬品の出土（「月岡所獲古器図」に記録、現在は吉井歴史民俗資料館保管）{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}}。 * [[嘉永]]6年（[[1853年]]）の矢野一貞『筑後将士軍談』に出土品の図示{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1925年]]（[[大正]]14年）刊行の『史蹟名勝天然紀念物調査報告書』第1輯で報告。 * [[1961年]]（[[昭和]]36年）2月17日、出土品が国の[[重要文化財]]に指定。 * [[1985年]]（昭和60年）、発掘調査。三重周濠を確認（吉井町教育委員会、[[1989年]]に報告）。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り史跡説明板。。 * 墳丘長：80メートル - 墳丘最下段の基壇状テラスを含めた場合は95メートル。 * 後円部 高さ：7メートル * 前方部 高さ：6メートル Tsukinooka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後円部墳頂の石棺覆屋 Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|後円部墳丘入り口 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === [[ファイル:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|月岡宮掘開記}}{{small|吉井歴史民俗資料館展示。}}]] * 筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。所有者は八幡神社、吉井歴史民俗資料館保管。1961年（昭和36年）2月17日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9830|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳出土品}}。 ** 金銅装眉庇付鉄胄 1頭 ** 金銅装臑当形鉄製品 一括 ** 金銅帯金具残闕 一括 ** 金銅胡簶残闕 1具分 ** 銅鏡 4面 ** 埴輪家残闕 1箇分 ** 附 発掘関係書類 1冊 === その他 === 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑

{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館保管、同企画展示時に撮影。}} == 関連施設 == * 吉井歴史民俗資料館（うきは市吉井町） - 月岡古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（うきは市教育委員会、2005年設置） * 筑後川流域装飾古墳同時公開解説シート * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[小林三郎 (考古学者)|小林三郎]]|year=1989|chapter=月岡古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=若宮古墳群|title=福岡県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]41|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490417|ref={{Harvid|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1925|chapter=日ノ岡、月ノ岡古墳|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/88785 史蹟名勝天然紀念物調査報告書]|volume=第1輯|publisher=福岡県|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1977|chapter=|title=吉井町誌|volume=第1巻|publisher=吉井町|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=月岡古墳 国指定重要文化財出土図録|series=|publisher=吉井町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=若宮古墳群1 -月岡古墳・塚堂古墳・日岡古墳-|series=吉井町文化財調査報告書第4集|publisher=吉井町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|title=若宮古墳群3 -月岡古墳-|series=吉井町文化財調査報告書第19集|publisher=吉井町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * 若宮古墳群 ** [[塚堂古墳]] ** [[日岡古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9830|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳出土品}} * [https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=1&id=68 筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳出土品] - 福岡県教育庁教育総務部文化財保護課「福岡県の文化財」 * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html 若宮古墳群（日岡・月岡・塚堂古墳）] - うきは市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:つきのおかこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:うきは市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:福岡県の重要文化財]]

It is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mound). While not Historic Site1 as a historic site, its burial goods are recognized as a National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Wakamiya Kofun Group !Kofun name !colspan=2|Mound type !burial !built !Historic Status !notes |- |'''Tsukinooka Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||95m||{{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
({{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}})||Mid-5th century||None||burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 |- |{{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||91m||Two horizontal stone chambers||Late 5th century||None|| |- |{{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||74m||One horizontal stone chamber||Early 6th century||National Historic Site||Decorated kofun |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Currently, the mound lies within the grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, with a shrine building (covering the stone sarcophagus) at its peak. The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} built is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet

{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} == Related Facilities == * Yoshii History and Folklore Museum (Ukiha, Fukuoka) – Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukinooka Kofun. == See Also == * Wakamiya Kofun Group ''' [[Tsukadō Kofun]] ''' [[Hioka Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{National Important designation1 Database|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳Excavated Artifacts}} * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Wakamiya Kofun Group (Hioka, Tsukinooka, Tsukadō)] – Ukiha City Website [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukioka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[ja:月岡古墳]] [[en:Tsukioka Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|月岡古墳|version=102672266|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Front part: Unknown 1 |burial goods = 8 sets of armor, bronze mirrors, ornaments, horse fittings, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |satellite_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |buried_person = (Hypothesized) {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan |imperial_tomb = |historic_sites_status = None |tangible_cultural_properties = burial goods (National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka |type = Zenpokoenfun-W |map_dot_label = Tsukinooka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukinooka Kofun''' (月岡古墳, also Tsukinooka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Yoshii Town, {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|ja|うきは市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mound). While not Historic Site1 as a historic site, its burial goods are recognized as a National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Wakamiya Kofun Group !Kofun name !colspan=2|Mound type !burial !built !Historic Status !notes |- |'''Tsukinooka Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||95m||{{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
({{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}})||Mid-5th century||None||burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 |- |{{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||91m||Two horizontal stone chambers||Late 5th century||None|| |- |{{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||74m||One horizontal stone chamber||Early 6th century||National Historic Site||Decorated kofun |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Currently, the mound lies within the grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, with a shrine building (covering the stone sarcophagus) at its peak. The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} built is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|Helmet fragment

{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} == Related Facilities == * Yoshii History and Folklore Museum (Ukiha, Fukuoka) – Preserves and displays burial goods from Tsukinooka Kofun. == See Also == * Wakamiya Kofun Group ''' [[Tsukadō Kofun]] ''' [[Hioka Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukinooka Kofun}} * {{National Important designation1 Database|筑後国浮羽郡月岡古墳Excavated Artifacts}} * [https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Wakamiya Kofun Group (Hioka, Tsukinooka, Tsukadō)] – Ukiha City Website {{citation needed|Tsukioka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|月岡古墳|version=102672266|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukiha]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukioka Kofun]] [[ja:月岡古墳]] [[en:Tsukioka Kofun]]

Constriction area: Unknown 1
Front part: Unknown 1 |burial goods = 8 sets of armor, bronze mirrors, ornaments, horse fittings, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |satellite_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |buried_person = (Hypothesized) {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan |imperial_tomb = |historic_sites_status = None |tangible_cultural_properties = burial goods (National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka |type = Zenpokoenfun-W |map_dot_label = Tsukinooka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukinooka Kofun''' (月岡古墳, also Tsukinooka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Yoshii Town, {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|ja|うきは市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mound). While not Historic Site1 as a historic site, its burial goods are recognized as a National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Wakamiya Kofun Group !Kofun name !colspan=2|Mound type !burial !built !Historic Status !notes |- |'''Tsukinooka Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||95m||{{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
({{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}})||Mid-5th century||None||burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 |- |{{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||91m||Two horizontal stone chambers||Late 5th century||None|| |- |{{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||74m||One horizontal stone chamber||Early 6th century||National Historic Site||Decorated kofun |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Currently, the mound lies within the grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, with a shrine building (covering the stone sarcophagus) at its peak. The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} built is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet

Front part: Unknown 1 |burial goods = 8 sets of armor, bronze mirrors, ornaments, horse fittings, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |satellite_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |buried_person = (Hypothesized) {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan |imperial_tomb = |historic_sites_status = None |tangible_cultural_properties = burial goods (National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka |type = Zenpokoenfun-W |map_dot_label = Tsukinooka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukinooka Kofun''' (月岡古墳, also Tsukinooka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Yoshii Town, {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|ja|うきは市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. It is part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group and features a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mound). While not Historic Site1 as a historic site, its burial goods are recognized as a National {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Wakamiya Kofun Group !Kofun name !colspan=2|Mound type !burial !built !Historic Status !notes |- |'''Tsukinooka Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||95m||{{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
({{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}})||Mid-5th century||None||burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 |- |{{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||91m||Two horizontal stone chambers||Late 5th century||None|| |- |{{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||74m||One horizontal stone chamber||Early 6th century||National Historic Site||Decorated kofun |}
[[File:Tsukinooka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear circular part of the mound}}]] Tsukinooka Kofun is located on a plateau south of the {{ill|Chikugo River|ja|筑後川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in southern Fukuoka Prefecture. It types part of the Wakamiya Kofun Group alongside {{ill|Tsukadō Kofun|ja|塚堂古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Hioka Kofun|ja|日岡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Currently, the mound lies within the grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, with a shrine building (covering the stone sarcophagus) at its peak. The southern side of the mound has been altered for residential use. Excavations of the stone chamber during the late Edo period yielded grave goods, and a typeal investigation was conducted in 1985. The mound is oriented along an east-west axis, with the front part facing west. It measures 80 meters in length but extends to 95 meters when including the basal terrace.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The mound features {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including figurative haniwa (human, house, waterfowl, lid-shaped, and armor-shaped). Surrounding the mound are three concentric moats, the innermost believed to have contained water.{{Sfn|解説シート}} The burial chamber is a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff. The sarcophagus interior is painted with vermilion.{{Sfn|解説シート}} Excavations in the 1980s identified additional burial facilities in the constriction area and front part, though these remain unstudied.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} built is dated to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically the mid-5th century. Among the Wakamiya Kofun Group, it predates both Tsukadō Kofun and Hioka Kofun. The sarcophagus design, rare in Kyushu, parallels those used for influential individuals in the Kinai region, indicating connections to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Based on the area’s name, "Ukiha," and the large amount of armor interred, it is hypothesized that the buried individual belonged to the {{ill|Matō no omi|ja|的臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan, which was involved in military and diplomatic efforts with the Korean Peninsula.{{Sfn|広報うきは2023}} burial goods recovered from the site were Historic Site1 as Important designation1 in 1961. The sarcophagus is preserved within the shrine building atop the rear circular mound and is opened for viewing on the third Saturday of each month. == History == * {{ill|Bunka (era)|ja|文化|1=Bunka|2=ja|3=文化 (元号)|4=de|5=Bunka (Ära)|6=ko|7=분카|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1805): Discovery and excavation by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine priest Anyamoto Ōi. burial goods were recorded in the "Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects," now preserved at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.{{Sfn|若宮古墳群（平凡社）|2004}} * {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6 (1853): Description of burial goods in Yanō Kazutada's "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan."{{Sfn|月岡古墳（古墳）|1989}} * 1925: First typeally reported in Volume 1 of the "Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Survey Report." * 1961: burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 by the Japanese government.{{Sfn|国指定}} * 1985: Excavation conducted, confirming triple moats. Findings were reported in 1989 by the Yoshii Town Board of Education. == burial goods == === Important designation1 (Nationally Designated) === [[File:月岡宮掘開記.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tsukinooka Inventory of Ancient Objects}}{{small|Displayed at the Yoshii History and Folklore Museum.}}]] * burial goods excavated from Tsukinooka Kofun (Chikugo Province, Ukiha District) – Archaeological materials preserved by Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. Designated on February 17, 1961.{{Sfn|国指定}} ''' Gold-bronze helmet with eyebrow ornament (1) ''' Gold-bronze shin guards (1 set) ''' Gold-bronze belt fittings (set) ''' Gold-bronze quiver fragments (1 set) ''' Bronze mirrors (4) ''' Haniwa house fragments (1) ''' Accompanying excavation documents (1 volume) 月岡古墳 金銅装小札鋲留眉庇付冑 (橿考研保管).JPG|Gold-bronze helmet
{{small|Preserved at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.}} 月岡古墳 鋲留眉庇付冑片 (橿考研保管).JPG|Helmet fragment
